Acheived goals: Reported continued progress under the 2019 Political Agreement for Peace and Reconciliation between the Government and 14 armed groups, including the dissolution of two additional armed groups and the signing of a third agreement for the return of the Movement of Central African Patriots to the process, facilitated by Chad.

Reported in March an Special Criminal Court (SCC) a war crimes court in the domestic justice system with national and international staff and assistance—arrested and charged Mohamed Ali Fadoul, A former Seleka armed group member charged with crimes against humanity and war crimes linked to the 2014 attack at the Notre-Dame church.In June, the SCC convicted six members of the Popular Front for the Renaissance of the Central African Republic (Front populaire pour la renaissance de la Centrafrique, FPRC) of war crimes and crimes against humanity.
Report of President signing a contract with Starlink to expand internet services to rural and remote regions, addressing a long-standing infrastructure gap.

Report of President Touadéra participating in the CEMAC extraordinary summit in January 2026, committing to fiscal discipline and aligning national finance laws with IMF-supported programs to ensure economic sovereignty.

Goals: Hold Historic local elections and finalization of legislative cycle
Acheived goals: 28 December 2025, President Touadéra secured his third term, and for the first time in decades, local mayors and regional councilors were elected.

Project includes finalizing the paving of key routes connecting Bangui to the Cameroonian border (the "lifeline" for CAR's imports) and modernizing the aging power grid in the capital and expanding solar-hybrid mini-grids to provincial "growth poles" like Berberati and Bambari.

Goals: Total audit of all mining concessions by April 2026
Acheived goals: Report of at least 15% of all mining revenue is reinvested directly into the local infrastructure of the specific community where the mine is located.

Goals: Unlock $50 million in additional budgetary support,an strict ''performance criteria'' set by the IMF and World Bank
Acheived goals: Report of government passing a ''transparency-first'' budget that moved several state-owned enterprise accounts into the central Treasury.
